summary.what: Pending detection: off-first-parent gate fallback + direct docSet membership filter
summary.why: Second reviewer caught two related gaps after Laura's case: gate's LogFirstParent walks a structurally weird range when latest.anchor is on a side branch; filterByRules used docSet only for revert detection, never for direct 'is this commit documented' membership. Both fixes were needed together — the gate fallback alone returned unfiltered reachable commits because direct docSet membership wasn't a thing. Adding it is correct in all cases and surfaces 'documented' as a distinct classify-reason in the TIMBERS_DEBUG trace and countAutoSkipped count
summary.how: Extracted anchorShortCircuit helper in diagnostic.go handles both stale-anchor and off-first-parent triggers, routing both to CommitsReachableFrom + filterCommits. Added docSet[commit.SHA] membership check in filterByRules before ack/revert checks. Split classifyCommit into classifyByIdentity and classifyByContent to manage complexity. Moved dropEmptyFileChanges/dropEmptyMerges to new filter_empties.go. Two new unit tests prove the gate fallback path is taken (asserts firstParentCalled=false in fallback scenario, =true in normal scenario)

notes: Reviewer's original recommendation said 'route through all-reachable + docSet' but assumed docSet was used for direct membership filtering. It wasn't — only for revert detection. Discovered by writing the test, which initially failed because the unfiltered reachable list came back unchanged. Realized the missing piece is direct membership filtering. Both fixes together get Laura's class to zero pending; either alone is insufficient. Refactoring into anchorShortCircuit helper + extracted classify helpers + filter_empties.go was driven by lint budget but reads cleaner than the inline original. Phase 1's algorithm change (timbers-okv) was correctly identified as unnecessary — the algorithm is fine, the filtering coverage was the gap
